name: auth
context: GitHub authentication — tokens, OAuth apps, GitHub Apps, and fine-grained permissions
info: GitHub supports several authentication methods: personal access tokens (classic and fine-grained), OAuth 2.0 apps, and GitHub Apps with installation tokens. Fine-grained PATs are recommended for automation — they scope to specific repos and permissions. GitHub Apps are preferred for production integrations — they act as their own identity and support webhooks, rate limit increases, and org-level installs.

depends.reason: Most auth is for repo access

author: dojo-community
